strategy.name: Golden Cross — SMA(50)/SMA(200) Trend Following
sources: Meb Faber — A Quantitative Approach to Tactical Asset Allocation
strategy.description: Two simple moving averages, one signal. Buy when SMA(50) crosses above SMA(200) (Golden Cross). Exit when SMA(50) crosses back below SMA(200) (Death Cross). Long-only. Daily bars. Designed for broad index ETFs — SPY, VFV, VOO.

indicators.sma_50: Short-term trend. 50-day simple moving average of close prices.
indicators.sma_200: Long-term trend. 200-day simple moving average of close prices.

long: SMA(50) is above SMA(200) — long-term uptrend confirmed
Yesterday SMA(50) was at or below SMA(200) — today is a fresh cross (Golden Cross)
We do not currently hold a position in this symbol

exit_rules: Exit when SMA(50) crosses back below SMA(200) (Death Cross)
No fixed stop loss — the moving average crossover IS the exit signal
risk_rules: Maximum trade size capped by MAX_TRADE_SIZE_USD in .env
Maximum trades per day capped by MAX_TRADES_PER_DAY in .env
Trade only during regular US market hours (9:30am–4:00pm ET, Mon–Fri)
Skip days with US market holidays
notes: Trend-following on broad indices. Expect 1–2 entry signals per year, occasional whipsaws. Underperforms buy-and-hold in steady bull markets, outperforms during bear markets. Do not apply to individual stocks without backtesting — single-name trend systems have very different risk profiles.
